reasoning: The informal statement asserts infinitely many primes p with p ≡ -1 (mod 4). The Lean statement says: for every N : ℕ, there exists p ≥ N that is prime with p + 1 ≡ 0 [MOD 4]. In Mathlib, `a ≡ b [MOD n]` unfolds to `a % n = b % n`, so `p + 1 ≡ 0 [MOD 4]` means `4 ∣ (p+1)`, i.e., p ≡ -1 (mod 4). For a subset of ℕ, being unbounded (∀ N, ∃ p ≥ N in the set) is equivalent to being infinite, so the quantifier structure correctly encodes "infinitely many". The formalization matches the informal claim exactly.
